architecture.summary: FlashMem distills latent memory from a frozen LLM backbone's transient reasoning states without auxiliary encoders. The system reads the final hidden state as a summary of interaction history, feeds it to a trainable Shared-KV Consolidator that attends directly to the frozen backbone's KV cache to synthesize memory, and uses a parameter-free Cognitive Monitor that measures attention entropy to adaptively trigger consolidation when epistemic uncertainty is high. Memory is injected back into the backbone as prepended tokens or KV cache entries for subsequent reasoning steps.

biblio.title: FlashMem: Distilling Intrinsic Latent Memory via Computation Reuse

claims.statement: FlashMem uses the last hidden state as a compact and empirically effective summary of the interaction history
The Shared-KV Consolidator synthesizes memory by attending directly to the backbone's frozen cache, eliminating redundant re-parameterization
A parameter-free Cognitive Monitor leverages attention entropy to adaptively trigger consolidation only when high epistemic uncertainty is detected
FlashMem reduces inference latency by 5 times compared to heavy baselines
FlashMem matches the performance of heavy baselines while reducing inference latency
